title: hooks/1.0.0: Error on empty process.args instead of panicking
body:
[The `process` property is optional][1], which this package already handled appropriately, although I've added a new test here to guard against regressions.

[The `process.args` entry is required when `process` is set][2], and it's also [required to contain at least one entry][3].  The previous implementation here assumed that would always be satisfied, and panicked on empty `process.args` (reported by @baude).  With this commit, we avoid the panic and instead return an error message explaining why the input was invalid.

[1]: https://github.com/opencontainers/runtime-spec/blame/v1.0.1/config.md#L145
[2]: https://github.com/opencontainers/runtime-spec/blame/v1.0.1/config.md#L157
[3]: https://github.com/opencontainers/runtime-spec/blame/v1.0.1/config.md#L158

I don't see any mentions of "ipcns" in our issues.  Should I file a new one?  Is that a host flake?  Buggy libpod code?

[1]: https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/libpod/b72bba0b4da89315e1449679580134ba76dee799.0.1527193715224464680/output.log

That's an `ipc=host` test that verifies that the host's `/dev/shm` is mounted into the container by making a temporary file there with `mktemp` on the host and checking for its existence with `ls` in a container. It doesn't look racy, but I suspect it is a flake of some sort.

:sunny: Test successful - [status-papr](https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/libpod/a7180cd5459ca063c14a60965b4487f04c0af439.1527199257940073728/index.html)
Approved by: mheon
Pushing a7180cd5459ca063c14a60965b4487f04c0af439 to master...
The merge tests succeeding further supports it being a flake.
